sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Dementia: assessment, management and support for people living with dementia and their carers
sources.version: NICE NG97; reviewed 2025-10-24 with no update required
sources.population: Adults with suspected or diagnosed dementia and their carers.
sources.clinical_setting: Initial assessment, specialist assessment, ongoing care coordination, and support.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult memory and cognitive concern assessment and support; partial for undifferentiated memory symptoms and not directly validated for UAE referral, driving, or social-care pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.11 — initial and specialist assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports patient and collateral history, cognitive, behavioural and psychological symptoms, daily-life impact, physical and neurological examination, appropriate blood or urine tests, validated cognitive tools, functional informant tools, reversible causes, rapid progression, and specialist assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.34–1.2.35 and 1.3.1–1.3.2 — monitoring, coordination, and support plans
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports flexible or prescheduled monitoring, review of emerging needs, a named coordinator, and documented care and support planning.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.6 and 1.11.1–1.11.8 — driving information and carer support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented driving information and review of carer information, assessment, education, and support needs.

archive.title: Dementia: assessment, management and support for people living with dementia and their carers
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.population: Adults with suspected or diagnosed dementia and their carers.
archive.setting: Initial assessment, specialist assessment, ongoing care coordination, and support.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.11 — initial and specialist assessment
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports patient and collateral history, cognitive, behavioural and psychological symptoms, daily-life impact, physical and neurological examination, appropriate blood or urine tests, validated cognitive tools, functional informant tools, reversible causes, rapid progression, and specialist assessment.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.34–1.2.35 and 1.3.1–1.3.2 — monitoring, coordination, and support plans
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports flexible or prescheduled monitoring, review of emerging needs, a named coordinator, and documented care and support planning.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.6 and 1.11.1–1.11.8 — driving information and carer support
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented driving information and review of carer information, assessment, education, and support needs.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Epilepsies in children, young people and adults
sources.population: Children, young people, and adults with suspected or established epilepsy.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, specialist, emergency, and ongoing epilepsy care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for epilepsy assessment, monitoring, information, emergency planning, and risk review; international only and local driving, referral, and prescribing rules require UAE review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.3.7 — seizure assessment, EEG, and imaging
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports seizure-event and witness history, physical examination, ECG or metabolic context, EEG review, MRI review, recurrence after remission, and specialist assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 2.1.1–2.1.11 — information, safety, and daily impact
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports trigger, adherence, side-effect, safety, driving, employment or education, reproductive-health, and care-plan discussions.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 4.1.1–4.5.11 — treatment, monitoring, and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports seizure-control and adverse-effect review, clinician-led medication and interaction review, antiseizure medication levels when indicated, care review, and pregnancy-related specialist review without mapping a dose.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 7.1.1–7.3.1 — status, cluster, and prolonged seizure plans
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documentation of seizure clusters or convulsive emergencies and a clinician-agreed individual emergency management plan without mapping emergency medication or doses.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 10.1.1–10.2.2 — epilepsy-related death and SUDEP risk
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports seizure frequency, adherence, alcohol or drug use, uncontrolled seizures, abnormal neurological findings, and clinician-documented risk and safety discussion.
